---
title: "Mapping Workflow"
author: "AY"
editor: source
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 4
    toc-expand: false
    number-sections: true
    number-depth: 4
---

This page documents the current mapping workflow implemented in `workflow/mapping.smk`.

## Overview

The mapping workflow now supports three aligners:

- `star`
- `bwa`
- `bowtie2`

The workflow:

1. validates the selected project, organism, FASTQ folder, output folder, and required genome resources
2. auto-detects the FASTQ naming pattern in the input directory
3. maps each sample with one or more selected mappers
4. indexes each BAM file with `samtools index`
5. creates bigWig coverage files with `bamCoverage`

If `star` is selected, the workflow also writes:

- `<sample>.counts.tab`
- `<sample>.SJ.out.tab`

## Source files

- [`workflow/mapping.smk`](/Users/yeroslaviz/Documents/Github/bcfngs_Mapping_automation/workflow/mapping.smk)
- [`configs/run.yaml`](/Users/yeroslaviz/Documents/Github/bcfngs_Mapping_automation/configs/run.yaml)
- [`configs/genomes.yaml`](/Users/yeroslaviz/Documents/Github/bcfngs_Mapping_automation/configs/genomes.yaml)
- [`scripts/bcfngs`](/Users/yeroslaviz/Documents/Github/bcfngs_Mapping_automation/scripts/bcfngs)

The recommended entry point is the wrapper CLI in `scripts/bcfngs`. It prepares the Snakemake command and exposes the most common runtime options as CLI flags.

## Required genome resources

Before running mapping, the genome preparation workflow must already have created the required indices and the chromosome size file under the configured `genomes_root`.

Depending on the selected mapper, `workflow/mapping.smk` checks for:

- `star`: `<genomes_root>/<organism>/starIndex/SA`
- `bwa`: `<genomes_root>/<organism>/bwaIndex/<organism>.bwt`
- `bowtie2`: `<genomes_root>/<organism>/bowtie2Index/<organism>.1.bt2`
- all runs: `<genomes_root>/<organism>.chromSize`

If any of these are missing, the workflow stops before submitting mapping jobs.

## Required configuration

The base per-project settings live in [`configs/run.yaml`](/Users/yeroslaviz/Documents/Github/bcfngs_Mapping_automation/configs/run.yaml):

```yaml
project: "P000"
path: "/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P000/P000_Testrun/conc.fastq/"
outdir: "/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/mapping"
organism: "Caenorhabditis_elegans"
threads: 16
ram_bytes: 168632718037
```

Important fields:

- `project`: used in the output path
- `path`: FASTQ input directory
- `outdir`: root output directory
- `organism`: organism key from [`configs/genomes.yaml`](/Users/yeroslaviz/Documents/Github/bcfngs_Mapping_automation/configs/genomes.yaml)
- `threads`: mapping threads per job
- `ram_bytes`: STAR BAM sort memory limit

Additional mapping settings are passed at runtime, usually via `./scripts/bcfngs mapping ...`:

- `--mapper star,bwa,bowtie2`
- `--paired-end` or `--single-end`
- `--bw-bin-size`
- `--bw-normalization`
- `--keep-intermediary-files`

## FASTQ pattern auto-detection

The workflow scans the FASTQ directory and accepts the first supported pattern it finds:

1. `*_R1.fastq.gz`
2. `*_R1_001.fastq.gz`
3. `*_L1_R1.fastq.gz`
4. `*.conc.R1.fastq.gz`

For paired-end runs, the matching `R2` files must also exist. If they do not, the workflow stops with an error before any mapping starts.

## Output layout

Outputs are written below:

```text
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/
```

Per mapper, the workflow writes:

```text
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/star/<sample>.bam
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/star/<sample>.bam.bai
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/star/bigwig/<sample>.bw
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/star/<sample>.counts.tab
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/star/<sample>.SJ.out.tab

<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/bwa/<sample>.bam
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/bwa/<sample>.bam.bai
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/bwa/bigwig/<sample>.bw

<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/bowtie2/<sample>.bam
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/bowtie2/<sample>.bam.bai
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/bowtie2/bigwig/<sample>.bw

<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/logs/
```

## Recommended usage with `bcfngs`

### Dry-run and validation

Use `plan` first. This validates config and inputs, runs Snakemake lint, and performs a dry-run.

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan \
  --mapper star \
  --paired-end \
  --project P000 \
  --organism Caenorhabditis_elegans \
  --fastq-dir /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P000/P000_Testrun/conc.fastq \
  --outdir /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/mapping
```

### Local run

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run \
  --mapper star \
  --paired-end \
  --project P000 \
  --organism Caenorhabditis_elegans \
  --fastq-dir /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P000/P000_Testrun/conc.fastq \
  --outdir /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/mapping \
  --executor local \
  --jobs 20
```

### SLURM run

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run \
  --mapper star,bwa \
  --paired-end \
  --project P000 \
  --organism Caenorhabditis_elegans \
  --fastq-dir /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P000/P000_Testrun/conc.fastq \
  --outdir /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/mapping \
  --executor slurm \
  --jobs 20 \
  --slurm-partition p.hpcl93 \
  --slurm-time 04:00:00 \
  --slurm-cpus-per-task 20
```

Useful runtime flags:

- `--mapper`: comma-separated mapper list
- `--single-end`: use single-end mode instead of the paired-end default
- `--threads`: override mapping threads from `configs/run.yaml`
- `--ram-bytes`: STAR sort RAM override
- `--bw-bin-size`: bigWig bin size
- `--bw-normalization`: `bamCoverage --normalizeUsing` mode

## Direct Snakemake usage

If needed, the workflow can also be run directly without the wrapper.

Dry-run:

```bash
snakemake \
  --printshellcmds \
  --dry-run \
  -s workflow/mapping.smk \
  --configfile configs/run.yaml \
  --config \
    project=P000 \
    organism=Caenorhabditis_elegans \
    fastq_dir=/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P000/P000_Testrun/conc.fastq \
    outdir=/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/mapping \
    mappers=star,bwa \
    read_mode=paired \
    threads=16 \
    ram_bytes=168632718037 \
    bw_bin_size=10 \
    bw_normalization=CPM
```

## Notes

- The workflow supports exactly one organism per run.
- Duplicate mapper names are removed automatically, but unknown mapper names are rejected.
- The wrapper defaults to paired-end mode, so use `--single-end` explicitly for single-end libraries.
- STAR temporary outputs are removed unless `--keep-intermediary-files` is set.
- `bamCoverage` is used directly on the BAM files; no intermediate wig files are created in this workflow.
